item.title: April 2026 Monthly Newsletter
item.description: Modern rights at work April saw the implementation of a number of measures from the Labour Government's landmark Employment Rights Act, a manifesto pledge at the 2024 General Election These changes are being introduced gradually across 2026 and 2027, allowing employers time to prepare and ensuring that workers have clear information about their new rights. This Bill represents the biggest reset to workers’ rights in a generation and, as previously happened with the National Minimum Wage, we...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Moving forward with Labour
item.description: Diana Johnson MP attended the official launch of Labour's Local Election campaign in Hull. Labour candidates have already been working hard in communities across Hull and they will do even more if elected on 7 May to work with the Labour Government to ease the cost of living crisis, safeguard vital local services and promote Hull's economic regeneration.
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: March 2026 Monthly Newsletter
item.description: An eventful month at home and abroad March saw the Chancellor's Spring Statement. This contained much positive news about Britain's economy - especially on inflation, growth and public finances. However, this soon overtaken by the outbreak of hostilities in Iran and around the Middle East that has caused another unwelcome energy price shock. Challenging failure in local NHS Trust Late March saw the announcement by the Health Secretary Wes Streeting that Hull University Teaching Hospitals NHS...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Hull MPs back Government intervention in Hull NHS Trust
item.description: Hull’s Labour MPs have welcomed the decision by the Health Secretary Wes Streeting to include Hull University Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust in the Government’s NHS Intensive Recovery programme. In a joint statement, Diana Johnson MP, Emma Hardy MP and Karl Turner MP said: “Following Hull MPs’ discussions with Health Ministers, it’s welcome that decisive action is being taken by the Secretary of State for Health to make Hull part of the NHS Intensive Recovery programme from April. “With Hull...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Hull MPs in ‘Ditch the Diesel’ call on Hull rail electrification
item.description: Writing in the Yorkshire Post today (Saturday 14 March), Hull’s three Labour MPs have issued a joint plea for the opportunity to be taken to secure Hull rail electrification. The full text of the article reads: Commenting further, Hull North and Cottingham Labour MP Diana Johnson said: “There is a great opportunity to use the latest train battery technology to have faster, greener and more economic-to-run rail services, saving the Government money, not just for Hull but across the...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Backing Lyla's Law
item.description: John and Emma Story, Diana Johnson and Health Secretary Wes Streeting Diana Johnson MP supported constituents John and Emma Story who came to Westminster promoting Lyla's Law on mandatory testing for all children presenting with potential Type 1 Diabetes symptoms. Lyla Story died in May 2025, fewer than 24 hours after her mother took her to a doctor, who diagnosed acute tonsillitis. An inquest into Lyla's death in September heard that she had undiagnosed Type 1 Diabetes. The Hull North and...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: The Humber holds the key to growing the UK economy
item.description: Diana Johnson MP addresses the Hull and Humber Chamber of Commerce Diana Johnson MP spoke today to the Annual General Meeting of Hull and Humber Chamber of Commerce at the MKM Stadium about the economic importance of the Humber. The Hull North and Cottingham MP told the meeting: "We will not achieve stronger UK economic growth, upon which so much else relies, without the Humber. "With our regional profile of carbon emitting industries, we will not achieve Net Zero without the Humber. "I know...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: February 2026 Monthly Newsletter
item.description: Preparing for the jobs of the Future While much of the media attention during February concerned various scandals and conflicts, going almost under the radar there were signs in improvement in everyday economic issues. The UK's economic growth continued to lead the G7, retail sales grew 4.5% over the year, Government finances recorded the biggest-ever surplus and inflation fell further - hopefully enabling further interest rate cuts to boost housing and businesses. Despite the recent...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Celebrating National Apprenticeships Week
item.description: Despite the recent encouraging news, there is clearly still much to do on combating the ongoing cost of living crisis and encouraging stronger economic growth. That's why it is so important to prepare people for the jobs of the future in a labour market that is expected to change with AI. This is important to me not just as a Hull constituency MP, but also in my role as Employment Minister. In February, the annual National Apprenticeships Week served as an important opportunity to highlight...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Labour action on child poverty
item.description: This week, while the Labour Government invested in taking 450,000 children out of poverty, Reform UK said that they would rather use the money to cut the price of a pint of beer by 5p. My article today for The Hull Story is below and at https://www.thehullstory.com/allarticles/diana-johnson-opinion-child-poverty . The vast majority of parents want to support their children through working for a living, paying their way and standing on their own two feet. Doing this has been challenging for...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: January 2026 Monthly Newsletter
item.description: Getting rail electrification on track Over the past 20 years, I have supported the cross-party campaigning, alongside the Humber business community, to electrify rail around the Humber. In 2013 there was even a privately-financed initiative to achieve it that was blocked by the then Conservative government in November 2016. The incoming Labour Government in 2024 was clear that to achieve higher levels of economic growth, we need every region and nation of the UK to play a part – and this...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Hull MPs Diana Johnson, Emma Hardy and Karl Turner have issued the following statement after our meeting yesterday with Rail Minister Lord Hendy
item.description: " In today’s meeting with the Rail Minister, Hull MPs welcomed the recent confirmation from the Department of Transport that electrification of the rail lines to Hull remains under consideration and discussed the options now available to make progress on it. “We noted that there is currently an opportunity to end the use of polluting and expensive-to-run diesel trains on TransPennine rail routes and boost capacity through a scheme of discontinuous electrification and new battery bi-modal...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Hull MPs Statement on Northern Powerhouse Rail
item.description: In response to today's Government announcement for plan for Northern Powerhouse Rail, Hull's MPs have issued the following joint statement about the electrification of rail lines to Hull. "We agree with the Government that for the UK to boost productivity and achieve higher economic growth the Humber, like the rest if the North, needs sustained investment in skills, jobs and in improving our transport connectivity and capacity - for passengers and freight. "It is clear from the Northern...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: 3,710 Children Across Hull North and Cottingham Set to Be Lifted Out of Poverty This April
item.description: An estimated 3,710 children across Hull North and Cottingham will be lifted out of poverty by Labour scrapping the two-child limit. New legislation introduced to Parliament today will see over half a million families across the country better off from April. Lifting the two-child limit on benefits such as Universal Credit comes alongside other action to cut the cost of living for families, including: Extending free school meals to half a million more children Cutting £150 off average...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: December 2025 Monthly Newsletter
item.description: A budget for working families The most important event during November was the Chancellor’s Autumn Budget. The impact of national and world events in recent years mean that many people are still finding it hard to make ends meet. This is why one of the main themes of the Budget was helping working families with the cost of living, along with protecting our NHS. Rail fares will be frozen for the first time in 30 years and the bus fares cap, so important around Hull, stays. We are...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Where to Get Help Over Christmas
item.description: Please be aware that the Parliamentary Christmas recess runs from 5pm on Friday 19 December 202 5 until 9am on Monday 5 January 202 6 . My emails will be infrequently monitored during this time and enquiries will be responded to in the New Year. Sources of Help and Information Over Christmas Hull City Council Services If you have an issue with Hull City Council services, you can call them on 01482 300 300 or visit the website: http://www.hull.gov.uk/council-and-democracy/customer-service/c...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Small Business Saturday Competition 2025 Winners Announced
item.description: Winners Blondes in Cottingham and Level in Hull North with runners-up Abigail Bakes and Sophistikate. Small Business Saturday took place on 6 December and this year my Small Business Saturday competition was back. I asked constituents to vote online for their favourite small businesses in Hull North and Cottingham. Level on Newland Avenue was voted as the favourite small business in Hull North. Open since 2010, Level has clearly made its mark in the local community. I was also pleased...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Christmas on the Cards
item.description: It’s almost that time of year again! For my Christmas card this year, I held a design competition amongst primary school pupils across Hull North and Cottingham. Pupils submitted wonderfully imaginative designs, but Sajidah from Thoresby Primary School created one that really stood out. I visited Thoresby to announce the news of Sajidah’s win and presented her with a goodie bag of local treats from Hull City Tigers, Hull Truck and Caffe Gelato. Despite her shock, I’m sure she was more...
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: GB energy invests in solar panels for two Hull North and Cottingham Schools
item.description: It’s brilliant news that Appleton Primary School and Sirius Academy North in Hull North and Cottingham will save money on their energy bills thanks to Great British Energy’s solar programme. This will cut energy bills for our local schools, meaning that more money can be put into crucial frontline services. That's the difference this Labour Government is making.
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson

item.title: Autism Support in Schools Westminster Hall Debate
item.description: With 1 in 8 young people not in education, employment or training – an issue particularly affecting many neurodivergent individuals – the importance of tailored training cannot be overstated. Excellent work is already taking place in our special schools - such as Northcott in my constituency. This approach should be replicated across the country.
item.dc:creator: Diana Johnson
